task: M5: implement the 2 final v1-target drivers (providers/qiniu + providers/upyun) — the DirectGrant non-URL milestone. Per provider_roadmap.md M5: validate that Signer.IssueDirectGrant covers both Token shape (Qiniu Upload Token + Download Token) AND Form shape (Upyun FORM upload params) without growing the frozen 4-mode set. Hard fence: NO new Code/Capability/DirectGrantMode (TestFrozenSurface stays 3-subtest green). Any abstraction-pressure point goes to docs/provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M5). Update matrix + CHANGELOG + RELEASING. Tag commands maintainer-gated.
scope_note: M5 = 2 providers in parallel via Ralph (same shape as M3 phase 2 + M4). After M5, all 10 v1-target providers are shipped at v0.1.0. M6 (stabilization, benchmarks, examples, OTel alignment) is next session work. v0.2.0 retrofit (12 architect optimization candidates accumulated across M3/M4 + httpx flake + Azure multipart metadata fix) is also queued.

stories.title: providers/qiniu native driver (qiniu/go-sdk/v7)
acceptanceCriteria: providers/qiniu/ scaffolded via scripts/add-provider.sh qiniu (go.mod with module github.com/slowfei/oss-client/providers/qiniu + parent + testkit requires + replace directives).
providers/qiniu/go.mod requires github.com/qiniu/go-sdk/v7 directly; no other cloud SDKs. Pin Go version that the SDK requires.

factory.go implements uos.Factory: Provider() = 'qiniu'; Validate requires DriverConfig with Region (Qiniu zone) OR custom Endpoint, plus CredentialProvider yielding qiniu.Credentials (AccessKey + SecretKey). AuthScheme is AuthCustom; document that Qiniu's Upload Token / Download Token / Manage Token are derived FROM the same AK/SK pair via different signing scopes (no need for distinct credential payloads in v0.1). Open builds the resourcec/storage clients with shared AK/SK; SDK's internal retry disabled if exposed.

driver.go implements full uos.Client + 4 sub-services using qiniu/go-sdk/v7. Use storage.BucketManager for bucket ops, storage.FormUploader / storage.ResumeUploaderV2 for multipart (or whichever vendor primitive maps cleanest onto MultipartService — document the choice). Bypasses pkg/uos/transfer.Manager. Metadata uses x-qn-meta-* on the wire (use s3common.LowerMetadataKeys for case-folding).

Signer.SignURL: URL-shaped private-bucket access via storage.MakePrivateURL (read access). For SignURL Write, return *uos.Error{Code: ErrUnsupported, Capability: CapSignedURLWrite, Reason: 'qiniu write authorization is non-URL; use IssueDirectGrant'} per matrix footnote 4.

**Signer.IssueDirectGrant** validates DirectGrantModeToken in a NEW context (Qiniu Upload Token, distinct from Azure SAS path). For DirectGrantRequest with Operation=upload (or equivalent — verify the exact field name in pkg/uos), build the Qiniu Upload Token via storage.PutPolicy + .UploadToken(creds), return *uos.DirectGrant{Mode: DirectGrantModeToken, Token: <upload-token>, URL: <upload-host-base>, Headers: nil}. For Operation=download, return Download Token via auth.SignToken or equivalent, also Mode=DirectGrantModeToken. **Hard fence**: if no existing DirectGrantMode value fits the Qiniu shape, STOP and log to provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M5).

error_map.go: qiniu/go-sdk surfaces errors via storage.ErrorInfo (HTTP-style with Code int + ErrInfo string). Use s3common.MapHTTPStatus + s3common.MapContextErr; vendor-specific reasons in a LOCAL switch in error_map.go. **DO NOT extend s3common.MapCodeString** (Qiniu is non-S3-family — same boundary as gcs/azure).

capabilities.go returns the 13-cell Report matching the qiniu column in docs/provider_matrix.md: 5 ✅ (BucketCRUD, ObjectCRUD, ListPrefixDelimiter, RangeRead, MultipartUpload) + 1 ✅ (SignedURLRead — read URL works) + 1 🟡⁴ (SignedURLWrite — Upload Token non-URL, returns ErrUnsupported with reason) + 1 ✅ DirectGrant (validates the M5 milestone goal) + 3 🧩⁷ (ObjectTagging, ObjectACL, ManagedEncryption — bespoke admin APIs not unified) + 1 ❌⁹ (Versioning — not exposed as data-plane capability) + 1 🧩¹² (NativeMove). Total = 13.

driver_test.go (//go:build docker — load-bearing): TestRunSuite SKIPs by default (Qiniu's wire dialect is non-S3 / non-MinIO compatible); cloud-nightly env vars OMC_QINIU_NIGHTLY_KEY (AccessKey) + OMC_QINIU_NIGHTLY_SECRET + OMC_QINIU_NIGHTLY_BUCKET + OMC_QINIU_NIGHTLY_ZONE gate the real-Qiniu contract suite. TestSpawnMinIOSmoke runs in PR gate (validates testkit wiring).
From providers/qiniu: go build / go vet / gofmt -l . silent. From root: TestFrozenSurface 3 subtests pass (M5 hard fence — no Code/Cap/DirectGrantMode added).

Report any abstraction-pressure point in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M5): notably whether DirectGrant.Token + DirectGrant.URL composition cleanly expresses the Qiniu Upload Token contract, and whether DirectGrantRequest.Operation enum (if it exists; otherwise ad-hoc field) is sufficient to distinguish upload vs download token issuance.

stories.title: providers/upyun native driver (upyun/go-sdk/v3 or REST)
acceptanceCriteria: providers/upyun/ scaffolded; go.mod requires github.com/upyun/go-sdk/v3 directly (or implement REST direct if v3 SDK has poor maintenance / dep cost). Pin Go version.

factory.go implements uos.Factory: Provider() = 'upyun'; Validate requires DriverConfig.Bucket (Upyun's 'service name' which is the unified bucket) plus CredentialProvider yielding Operator name + password (or signature key). AuthScheme default AuthCustom (signature auth preferred); AuthSharedKey accepted as basic-auth fallback (documented as discouraged). Open builds the upyun.UpYun client.

driver.go implements full uos.Client + 4 sub-services. Use upyun primitives end-to-end: Get/Put/Delete/Mkdir/List for object plane; resumable upload via Put with chunk uploading or REST PUT-with-Range for multipart (whichever maps cleanest — document choice). Bypasses pkg/uos/transfer.Manager. Metadata: Upyun uses x-upyun-meta-* on the wire (s3common.LowerMetadataKeys for case-folding).

Signer.SignURL (download): URL-shaped private-bucket signed download URL via upyun's signature mechanism — concatenates expiration, full path, and signature into query string per matrix footnote 3. Returns *uos.SignedURL with Method=GET only.

Signer.SignURL (upload): per matrix footnote 3, upload signed URLs are issued as FORM authorization, NOT URL. Return *uos.Error{Code: ErrUnsupported, Capability: CapSignedURLWrite, Reason: 'upyun upload authorization is FORM-based; use IssueDirectGrant'}.

**Signer.IssueDirectGrant** validates DirectGrantModeForm — the LAST frozen DirectGrantMode value not yet exercised by any shipped driver. For DirectGrantRequest (write operation), build the Upyun FORM authorization params (policy base64-encoded JSON + signature), return *uos.DirectGrant{Mode: DirectGrantModeForm, Token: '', URL: '<upload-endpoint>', Headers: map containing 'Authorization': '<signature>', and FormFields: map containing 'policy', 'date', etc. — verify the exact pkg/uos.DirectGrant struct supports a 'FormFields' or equivalent field BEFORE implementing}. **Hard fence**: if DirectGrant struct lacks a way to express FORM POST fields cleanly (e.g., no FormFields map), STOP and log to provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M5) as a v0.2.0 widening candidate. Do NOT add a new field to pkg/uos.DirectGrant in this milestone.

error_map.go: upyun errors come back as *upyun.Error (HTTP-style with Code int + Msg string) or wrapped errors. Use s3common.MapHTTPStatus + s3common.MapContextErr; vendor codes in LOCAL switch. **DO NOT extend s3common.MapCodeString** (Upyun is non-S3-family).

capabilities.go returns the 13-cell Report matching the upyun column: 5 ✅ (BucketCRUD, ObjectCRUD, ListPrefixDelimiter, RangeRead, MultipartUpload) + 2 🟡³ (SignedURLRead — works for download; SignedURLWrite — FORM via DirectGrant per footnote 3) + 1 ✅ DirectGrant (Mode=Form validation moment) + 3 🧩⁷ (ObjectTagging, ObjectACL, ManagedEncryption — bespoke admin APIs) + 1 ❌⁹ (Versioning) + 1 🧩¹² (NativeMove). Total = 13. Note: Upyun media-processing / persistent-pipeline features are explicitly out-of-scope for v1; reach via As(target) only.

driver_test.go (//go:build docker): TestRunSuite SKIPs by default (Upyun's REST/signature dialect is non-S3); cloud-nightly env vars OMC_UPYUN_NIGHTLY_BUCKET + OMC_UPYUN_NIGHTLY_OPERATOR + OMC_UPYUN_NIGHTLY_PASSWORD gate the real-Upyun contract suite. TestSpawnMinIOSmoke runs in PR gate.
From providers/upyun: go build / go vet / gofmt -l . silent. TestFrozenSurface still passes.

Report any abstraction-pressure point in provider_roadmap.md Lessons (M5): especially whether DirectGrant struct's existing fields (Mode/Token/URL/Headers) cleanly express FORM POST upload (which needs both signature header AND form field collection — distinct from a bearer token). This is the M5 abstraction question.

stories.title: Matrix + CHANGELOG + RELEASING + provider_roadmap (M5 lessons) update
acceptanceCriteria: docs/provider_matrix.md: qiniu + upyun driver-status rows updated to 'Shipped (v0.1.0)'.

docs/provider_matrix.md: 13 capability rows × 2 columns (qiniu + upyun) updated from ⏳M5 placeholders to actual symbols matching each driver's capabilities() return. Both columns gain ✅ on DirectGrant — the M5 validation moment.
docs/provider_matrix.md: 'Capability matrix' section grep ⏳M5 → 0 matches confirms full sweep (M5 is the last milestone before M6 stabilization, so all 13 cap rows × 10 columns must now be filled).

CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] gains entries for providers/qiniu/v0.1.0 and providers/upyun/v0.1.0 — each summarising SDK choice, the AuthCustom + DirectGrant model, the multipart-mapping decision, and the validation outcome on DirectGrantModeToken (Qiniu) / DirectGrantModeForm (Upyun).
RELEASING.md §4: provider tag commands extended to include providers/qiniu/v0.1.0 and providers/upyun/v0.1.0.

docs/provider_roadmap.md 'Lessons (M5)' section filled in (replace the '_(to be filled)_' placeholder). MUST honestly state: (a) whether Qiniu Upload Token + Download Token both fit DirectGrantModeToken cleanly; (b) whether Upyun FORM upload fits DirectGrantModeForm cleanly with the existing DirectGrant struct fields, OR whether a v0.2.0 widening (e.g., FormFields map) is recommended; (c) overall v1 abstraction validation verdict — 10 drivers shipped with zero / minimal pkg/uos change, OR specific frozen-surface gaps documented.
AGENTS.md Appendix A: add new item 8 capturing M5 outcome (10/10 v1-target providers shipped; DirectGrantMode 4-set fully exercised; v1 abstraction validation complete).
go.work updated to list the 2 new modules (root + testkit + 6 S3-family + gcs + azure + qiniu + upyun = 11 modules).
Final root-level go test -short -race ./... still green; TestFrozenSurface 3 subtests still pass.
